title: campanula macrorhiza subsp. macrorhiza
intro: Campanula macrorhiza subsp. macrorhiza, also known as tall bellflower, is a perennial plant species native to North America, characterized by its bell-shaped flowers and edible roots. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Campanula macrorhiza subsp. macrorhiza include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil to harness its medicinal properties.
